concepts.name: Queue
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure that follows the First-In, First-Out (FIFO) principle.

concepts.name: FIFO
concepts.short_definition: The principle where the first element added to a structure is the first one to be removed.

concepts.name: Enqueue
concepts.short_definition: The operation of adding an element to the rear of the queue.

concepts.name: Dequeue
concepts.short_definition: The operation of removing an element from the front of the queue.

concepts.name: Front
concepts.short_definition: The operation of viewing the element at the front of the queue without removing it.

concepts.name: Rear
concepts.short_definition: The position in the queue where new elements are added.

concepts.name: Empty Queue
concepts.short_definition: A queue that contains no elements.

concepts.name: Full Queue
concepts.short_definition: A queue that has reached its maximum capacity (relevant for array-based implementations).

concepts.name: Queue Operations
concepts.short_definition: The set of standard actions that can be performed on a queue.

concepts.name: Abstract Data Type
concepts.short_definition: A logical description of a data structure, defining its operations and behavior.

concepts.name: Linear Data Structure
concepts.short_definition: A data structure where elements are arranged in a sequential manner.

concepts.name: Array Implementation
concepts.short_definition: Implementing a queue using an array.

concepts.name: Linked List Implementation
concepts.short_definition: Implementing a queue using a linked list.

concepts.name: Circular Queue
concepts.short_definition: A queue implementation that uses a fixed-size array and wraps around.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: The measure of the time required to execute an algorithm as a function of the input size.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: The measure of the memory required to execute an algorithm as a function of the input size.

concepts.name: Applications of Queues
concepts.short_definition: Real-world scenarios and algorithms where queues are used.

concepts.name: Breadth-First Search
concepts.short_definition: A graph traversal algorithm that uses a queue to explore nodes level by level.

concepts.name: Operating System Scheduling
concepts.short_definition: Using queues to manage processes waiting for CPU time.

concepts.name: Printer Spooling
concepts.short_definition: Managing print jobs in the order they are submitted.

concepts.name: Call Center Systems
concepts.short_definition: Handling incoming calls in a first-come, first-served manner.

concepts.name: Data Buffering
concepts.short_definition: Temporarily storing data between processes with different speeds.

concepts.name: Array
concepts.short_definition: A contiguous block of memory storing elements of the same data type.

concepts.name: Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure where elements are linked using pointers.

concepts.name: Node
concepts.short_definition: A fundamental unit in a linked list, containing data and a pointer to the next node.

concepts.name: Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A variable that stores the memory address of another variable.

concepts.name: Capacity
concepts.short_definition: The maximum number of elements a queue can hold.

concepts.name: Size
concepts.short_definition: The current number of elements in a queue.

concepts.name: Dynamic Array
concepts.short_definition: An array that can resize itself automatically.

concepts.name: Static Array
concepts.short_definition: An array with a fixed size determined at compile time.

concepts.name: Head Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer that points to the front of the queue.

concepts.name: Tail Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer that points to the rear of the queue.

concepts.name: Data Element
concepts.short_definition: The individual item stored within a queue.

concepts.name: Sequential Access
concepts.short_definition: Accessing elements in a data structure one after another in order.

concepts.name: Queue Interface
concepts.short_definition: A contract defining the methods a queue must support.

concepts.name: Graph
concepts.short_definition: A data structure representing a set of objects and the connections between them.

concepts.name: Process Management
concepts.short_definition: Managing the execution of computer programs.

concepts.name: Task Scheduling
concepts.short_definition: Determining the order in which tasks are executed.

concepts.name: Message Queues
concepts.short_definition: A form of asynchronous communication between software components.
